entry.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
entry.content:
The critical, three-stage attack is now patched, but it's part of a new group of AI prompt-injection issues that use hidden URLs and other variables.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: HTTP/2 Bomb Attacks Put Telcos, Healthcare Orgs at Risk
entry.content:
The denial-of-service (DoS) exploit takes advantage of two features in HTTP/2 that were designed to save Internet bandwith, not power massive amplification attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'Lorem Ipsum' Malware Pivots to ClickFix Delivery
entry.content:
New analysis shows the campaign, which uses compromised WordPress sites, may be linked to the ransomware and data extortion group Vice Society.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
entry.content:
An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
entry.content:
Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
entry.content:
And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
entry.content:
"Shield-6G" will combine AI threat detection, digital twins, honeypots, and more, to help carriers protect 6G networks against the threats of tomorrow.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Get Out of Security Debt by Tackling the Exposure Problem
entry.content:
Teams digging out of security debt need to answer only two simple questions: Which vulnerabilities in our systems are exposed, and how long should they stay that way?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
entry.content:
Klue's Battlecards is now the third integrated application that has been compromised to steal customers' Salesforce data, and victims include Huntress, the cybersecurity vendor.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Operation Escaneo Signals Shift in LatAm Threat Landscape
entry.content:
The threat group's curious business model may combine opportunistic monetization alongside intel collection, without much coordination between the two.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
entry.content:
A leaked GitHub token underscores what most organizations get wrong: Treating secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity problem.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: No Exploits Required
entry.content:
Four decades of incident response experience suggest that exploits are often the symptom, not the root cause, of today’s cybersecurity failures. The post No Exploits Required appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Accenture to Acquire Majority Stake in Dragos, All of runZero, NetRise in $4.1 Billion OT Cybersecurity Push
entry.content:
The deal values industrial cybersecurity giant Dragos at $3.25 billion, and runZero and NetRise will operate under Dragos. The post Accenture to Acquire Majority Stake in Dragos, All of runZero, NetRise in $4.1 Billion OT Cybersecurity Push appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure
entry.content:
CISA has given federal agencies only three days to patch CVE-2026-20253, which can be exploited for unauthenticated remote code execution. The post Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC
entry.content:
WideField will accelerate Agentic SOC capabilities by expanding the lens on threat investigation to include identity, credentials, sessions, and blast radius. The post Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
entry.content:
The hackers exfiltrated data from Salesforce instances of Klue customers, such as Huntress and Recorded Future. The post Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: EU grants Ukraine access to cybersecurity reserve for major attacks
entry.content:
As Kyiv takes steps toward formal accession to the EU, the bloc is integrating Ukraine with its pool of pre-approved cybersecurity incident response companies.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hostile states behind three-quarters of attacks on Britain's critical infrastructure, cyber chief warns
entry.content:
NCSC CEO Richard Horne warned that “kinetic targeting in any conflict tomorrow will be based on intelligence gathered today” and that nation-state adversaries were “prepositioning” throughout British critical infrastructure.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack
entry.content:
Mackay Sugar said it was "working urgently" to verify claims that a highly active ransomware group was behind a cyberattack that shut down harvesting and milling operations.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
entry.content:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
entry.content:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
entry.content:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
entry.content:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
entry.content:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
entry.content:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026
entry.content:
The HIPAA Journal has compiled healthcare data breach statistics from October 2009, when the Department of Health and Human Services […] The post Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Hackers Claim Responsibility for Novo Nordisk Cyberattack
entry.content:
A hacking group has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on the pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk and says it exfiltrated more […] The post Hackers Claim Responsibility for Novo Nordisk Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: HIPAA Compliance for Medical Spas
entry.content:
Medical spas that collect health histories, administer injectable treatments, perform laser procedures, bill electronically for covered health care services, or […] The post HIPAA Compliance for Medical Spas appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: HIPAA Training for Medical Spas
entry.content:
Medical spas that qualify as HIPAA-Covered Entities should provide all members of their workforce with HIPAA training that covers foundational […] The post HIPAA Training for Medical Spas appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Heart Monitoring Device Manufacturer Discloses Cyberattack; Data Breach
entry.content:
iRhythm Holdings Inc., a publicly traded heart monitoring device manufacturer, has notified the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) about […] The post Heart Monitoring Device Manufacturer Discloses Cyberattack; Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: HIPAA Violation Cases: Types & Consequences
entry.content:
HIPAA violation cases are compliance investigations that result from a data breach being reported to the Department of Health and […] The post HIPAA Violation Cases: Types & Consequences appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: HIPAA Violation Fines
entry.content:
HIPAA violation fines can be issued by the Department of Health and Human Services’ Office for Civil Rights (OCR) and […] The post HIPAA Violation Fines appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Spencer Gifts Pays $450,000 Penalty to Resolve HIPAA Failures
entry.content:
The national retail company Spencer Gifts LLC has agreed to a $450,000 settlement to resolve alleged violations of the HIPAA […] The post Spencer Gifts Pays $450,000 Penalty to Resolve HIPAA Failures appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Active FortiBleed Campaign Impacting Fortinet Devices Across 194 Countries
entry.content:
From Arctic Wolf: Summary In mid-June 2026, security researchers identified an active, large-scale credential compromise campaign affecting Fortinet FortiGate firewalls, dubbed FortiBleed. Threat actors have been systematically extracting configuration files from internet-facing FortiGate devices and cracking the stored credential hashes, resulting in verified working administrator credentials for between 30,000 and 75,000 devices across 194 countries.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Data analysis of the Global Schools Group breach, Part 1
entry.content:
This is the first part of a two-part report of findings from the Global Schools Group data breach. All statistical analyses and findings were provided to DataBreaches by FulcrumSec, and are presented to assist those investigating the breach as well as parents and employees who might be concerned as to what types of data were... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Cybersecurity breach includes Crime Stoppers of Hamilton data
entry.content:
The Navigate360 (“P3”) data breach seems to finally be getting some attention in Canada. Nicole O’Reilly reports: Hamilton police say they’ve been made aware that a cybersecurity incident earlier this year affecting a U.S.-based online platform includes a breach of Crime Stoppers of Hamilton data. The P3 platform, owned by Navigate360, is under contract with... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Data analysis of the Global Schools Group breach, Part 2
entry.content:
In Part 1, DataBreaches published some totals and aggregate data from the recent Global Schools Group data breach. All analyses and statistics were provided to this site by FulcrumSec, who had attacked Global Schools Group (GSG) and exfiltrated the data. Data from three of GSG’s school brands were included in Part 1. Data for the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: UK: More than one year later, HCRG is first notifying patients of ransomware attack
entry.content:
In February 2025, after the Medusa ransomware gang claimed responsibility for an attack on the UK healthcare provider HCRG Care Group, HCRG confirmed it had been breached but would only say it was investigating. While they remained silent, SuspectFile obtained and reported on data provided to them by Medusa. SuspectFiles‘s reporting made it clear that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: HHS’ Oﬃce for Civil Rights Settles Ransomware Investigation with Spencer Gifts Health Plan for $450k, Corrective Action Plan
entry.content:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), Office for Civil Rights (OCR) today announced a settlement with Spencer Gifts LLC Flexible Beneﬁts and Welfare Beneﬁt Plans (the Plan), the employer-sponsored group health plan of Spencer Gifts LLC, a national retail company, over potential violations of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Texas government data breach allowed hackers to steal 3 million driver’s licenses and passports
entry.content:
Zack Whittaker reports: A data breach at a Texas state government department allowed hackers to take the driver’s license information and passport numbers of more than 3 million people, according to the state’s attorney general. The incident is one of the largest data breaches to affect the state this year. In a data breach notice on the Texas... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Amazon-Owned One Medical Faces Alleged 8.8TB Data Breach
entry.content:
Emily Hill reports: One Medical, the primary care provider acquired by Amazon in 2023, is facing questions after the cybercriminal group ShinyHunters claimed it stole 8.8 terabytes of company data and threatened to publish the information unless negotiations begin by June 22. The allegation remains unverified, and the group has not released any sample data to support... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Blue Fish Pediatrics notifies 41,485 Texans about data breach last year
entry.content:
Ahmed Humble reports that 41,485 Texans may have had personal and protected health information exposed in a data breach involving a Houston-based Blue Fish Pediatrics. The breach reportedly occurred between July 11 and July 17, 2025, but patients are only first being notified now. The types of information included: Full names Dates of birth Social... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Canadian hacker pleads guilty to charges for cyberattack on Texas Republican website
entry.content:
Alexandra Posadzki reports: Canadian hacker Aubrey Cottle has pleaded guilty to three charges stemming from a cyberattack linked to notorious hacktivist group Anonymous on the Texas Republican Party. Mr. Cottle, who appeared in court in Newmarket, Ont., on Thursday, pleaded guilty to fraudulently obtaining a computer service, namely the systems of web-hosting company Epik, causing mischief... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Leak confirms OpenAI is testing a ChatGPT for Science subscription
entry.content:
OpenAI appears to be testing a new subscription and experience for science use cases, but it's unclear if it'll be available to everyone regardless of their background. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Telegram admits it couldn't police exam-leak channels, India tells court
entry.content:
India's government has told the Delhi High Court that Telegram was warned about two weeks before it was blocked, and that the platform admitted it could not proactively detect the channels selling leaked exam papers. Telegram says it cooperated and the ban is unlawful. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations
entry.content:
Apple has released security updates to patch a high-severity flaw affecting the Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds that could allow attackers in Bluetooth range to spy on users' conversations. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
entry.content:
A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
entry.content:
Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack that distributed infected releases to paying customers via the vendor's official update system. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
entry.content:
Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Nintendo confirms data stolen in WebMD subsidiary cyberattack
entry.content:
Nintendo of America has confirmed to BleepingComputer that threat actors stole survey data from the third-party TinyPulse service used internally, but its systems were not compromised. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Gentlemen ransomware uses multiple EDR killers to disable defenses
entry.content:
The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers to help affiliates evade detection in attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak
entry.content:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) urged Fortinet customers to secure their devices after nearly 74,000 firewall and VPN credentials were exposed in a data leak dubbed "FortiBleed." [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
